channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Families across the U.S. are getting college acceptance letters — and tuition bills
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with economist Judith Scott-Clayton about the cost of college in the U.S. They discuss the difference between sticker and net price and the opaqueness of tuition costs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with economist Judith Scott-Clayton about the cost of college in the U.S. They discuss the difference between sticker and net price and the opaqueness of tuition costs.

item.title: How farmers are dealing with the fallout from the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe checks in Josh Gackle, former chairman of the American Soybean Association, on the state of the farming industry during the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe checks in Josh Gackle, former chairman of the American Soybean Association, on the state of the farming industry during the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: How the war in Iran is reshaping investor perceptions of Trump policies
item.description: Investors thought they had President Trump figured out as someone who would always back down from his most extreme policies. The war with Iran is challenging that.
item.content:encoded: Investors thought they had President Trump figured out as someone who would always back down from his most extreme policies. The war with Iran is challenging that.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: A legal scholar explains the challenges with Trump's global tariffs
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks to Ilya Somin, law professor at George Mason University and Cato Institute chair in constitutional studies, about the latest case challenging President Trump's global tariffs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon talks to Ilya Somin, law professor at George Mason University and Cato Institute chair in constitutional studies, about the latest case challenging President Trump's global tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly 2 years as energy costs spike
item.description: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.
item.content:encoded:
Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Inflation climbs to highest level in nearly 2 years
item.description: The federal government delivered a cost-of-living report Friday. A spike in gasoline prices triggered by the war with Iran contributed to inflation reaching its highest level in nearly two years.
item.content:encoded: The federal government delivered a cost-of-living report Friday. A spike in gasoline prices triggered by the war with Iran contributed to inflation reaching its highest level in nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Well-timed bets on Polymarket tied to the Iran war draw calls for investigations from lawmakers
item.description: Calls are increasing inside Congress for investigations into the prediction market platform Polymarket after the latest instance where groups of anonymous traders made strategic, well-timed bets on a major geopolitical event hours before it occurred.
item.content:encoded:
Calls are increasing inside Congress for investigations into the prediction market platform Polymarket after the latest instance where groups of anonymous traders made strategic, well-timed bets on a major geopolitical event hours before it occurred.

(Image credit: Amanda Andrade-Rhoades)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How an ancient resin traded for centuries got snarled up by the Iran war
item.description: It's not just energy supplies that have been disrupted by the Iran war. It's also hitting frankincense, a commodity that's been defining trade routes in the Middle East for thousands of years.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just energy supplies that have been disrupted by the Iran war. It's also hitting frankincense, a commodity that's been defining trade routes in the Middle East for thousands of years.

(Image credit: Maha Loubaris)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: How a cheese sandwich at the Masters is still $1.50 in an era of price hikes
item.description: The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.
item.content:encoded:
The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.

(Image credit: Andrew Redington)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Financial markets welcome Iran cease-fire but caution remains
item.description: Stocks rallied Wednesday on news of a two-week cease fire in the U.S. war with Iran. It could take some time, though, before drivers feel relief at the gas pump.
item.content:encoded: Stocks rallied Wednesday on news of a two-week cease fire in the U.S. war with Iran. It could take some time, though, before drivers feel relief at the gas pump.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
